channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: Intel and AMD sign longer-term server CPU deals with Chinese customers as prices rise
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Intel and AMD are signing longer-term purchase commitments with Chinese server customers for data-center processors as prices rise. Some agreements cover about one year, while the two companies have discussed commitments of two years or longer with some customers, according to people familiar with the talks. Server CPU prices in China have risen by more […]
item.content:encoded:
Intel and AMD are signing longer-term purchase commitments with Chinese server customers for data-center processors as prices rise. Some agreements cover about one year, while the two companies have discussed commitments of two years or longer with some customers, according to people familiar with the talks.

Server CPU prices in China have risen by more than 10% month on month for some products, one source said. The demand comes as AI data centers require CPUs alongside accelerators for server, storage, networking and inference workloads. [Reuters]

item.title: Chinese automakers take 34% of Europe’s plug-in hybrid deliveries in June
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese automakers, including BYD and Chery, accounted for a record 34% of plug-in hybrid vehicle deliveries in Europe in June, according to Dataforce data. Chinese brands also represented 11% of all new-car sales in Europe and 15% of battery-electric vehicle deliveries during the month. The 34% figure refers specifically to plug-in hybrid deliveries, which combine […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese automakers, including BYD and Chery, accounted for a record 34% of plug-in hybrid vehicle deliveries in Europe in June, according to Dataforce data.

Chinese brands also represented 11% of all new-car sales in Europe and 15% of battery-electric vehicle deliveries during the month. The 34% figure refers specifically to plug-in hybrid deliveries, which combine a battery with a gasoline engine. [Bloomberg]

item.title: TikTok tests paid microdrama app LimeShorts in the US
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: TikTok is testing a paid microdrama app called LimeShorts in the US, with the test starting in March. Users can pay $20 per week or $200 per year for full access, or buy virtual coins to unlock individual episodes. The app focuses on one- to five-minute vertical dramas across genres including comedy, romance and mystery. […]
item.content:encoded:
TikTok is testing a paid microdrama app called LimeShorts in the US, with the test starting in March. Users can pay $20 per week or $200 per year for full access, or buy virtual coins to unlock individual episodes.

The app focuses on one- to five-minute vertical dramas across genres including comedy, romance and mystery. Its content currently comes from third-party partners. LimeShorts has replaced ByteDance’s Mytopia app in the US, while TikTok is also operating the free PineDrama app and testing a dedicated microdrama feed within its main app. [IT Home, in Chinese]

item.title: Alibaba reportedly tests standalone Qwen Office product
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Alibaba is reportedly testing a product called Qwen Office internally, positioning it as a workplace AI product separate from Tongyi Qianwen. The product is focused on intelligent collaboration for office workflows and may be released in the near term. Alibaba is also hiring solution architects, business development specialists and AI product managers for the project. […]
item.content:encoded:
Alibaba is reportedly testing a product called Qwen Office internally, positioning it as a workplace AI product separate from Tongyi Qianwen. The product is focused on intelligent collaboration for office workflows and may be released in the near term.

Alibaba is also hiring solution architects, business development specialists and AI product managers for the project. The company has been consolidating QoderWork, Wukong and MuleRun into an enterprise productivity product under DingTalk CEO Chen Yusen. [Lanjinger, in Chinese]

item.title: Honor confirms August launch for Robot Phone with 4DoF gimbal
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Honor has confirmed that its Robot Phone will launch in August, bringing a four-degree-of-freedom mechanical gimbal to the top of a smartphone. The titanium-alloy gimbal uses miniature motors, with a volume that is 70% smaller than mainstream solutions, according to the company. The phone will use Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chip and introduce […]
item.content:encoded:
Honor has confirmed that its Robot Phone will launch in August, bringing a four-degree-of-freedom mechanical gimbal to the top of a smartphone. The titanium-alloy gimbal uses miniature motors, with a volume that is 70% smaller than mainstream solutions, according to the company.

The phone will use Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chip and introduce the core of Honor’s AgenticOS multimodal agent operating system. Honor has also opened reservations for the device, whose camera module can move independently from the phone body. [IT Home, in Chinese]

item.title: ByteDance’s PICO changes head as it prepares new MR device
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: ByteDance’s PICO XR business has appointed Li Xiaokai as its new head after founder Zhou Hongyi stepped down for personal career planning. The leadership transition is not expected to change PICO’s existing strategy, research and development work or product plans. PICO plans to launch a new mixed-reality device this year under the codename Project Swan. […]
item.content:encoded:
ByteDance’s PICO XR business has appointed Li Xiaokai as its new head after founder Zhou Hongyi stepped down for personal career planning. The leadership transition is not expected to change PICO’s existing strategy, research and development work or product plans.

PICO plans to launch a new mixed-reality device this year under the codename Project Swan. The device is expected to use a MicroOLED display and PICO’s self-developed MR chip, with a dual-chip design and system perception latency of 12 milliseconds.

Li joined PICO in 2021 and has overseen core product and technology work. PICO OS 6 expands the platform beyond VR applications to spatial computing, with support for Android apps, browsers, 3D content, OpenXR and WebXR. [36Kr, in Chinese]

item.title: Microsoft reportedly evaluates Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 for Copilot
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Microsoft is internally testing Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 and evaluating whether some Copilot inference requests currently handled by OpenAI and Anthropic models could be moved to Kimi K3, The Information reported. Microsoft has not made a final decision on replacing any models. Microsoft estimates that shifting part of Copilot’s workload to Kimi K3 could reduce […]
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft is internally testing Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 and evaluating whether some Copilot inference requests currently handled by OpenAI and Anthropic models could be moved to Kimi K3, The Information reported. Microsoft has not made a final decision on replacing any models.

Microsoft estimates that shifting part of Copilot’s workload to Kimi K3 could reduce annual cloud infrastructure costs by up to $600 million. The evaluation reflects Microsoft’s broader effort to use multiple AI models and reduce its dependence on a single supplier while controlling the cost of serving large volumes of AI requests.

Kimi K3 is reportedly being assessed not only for its performance in coding and reasoning, but also for its cost efficiency in specific Copilot tasks. Any deployment would still involve technical integration, data-sovereignty, security, and export-control considerations, and could initially be limited to less sensitive workloads. [The Information]

item.title: iQIYI launches China’s first licensed AIGC online story film
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese streaming platform iQIYI has launched Qitan: Paper Blade Across the Wasteland (《奇谭：纸刃渡荒墟》), which it describes as the first AIGC online story film in China to receive a Network Drama/Film Distribution License and go online. The film runs for more than 60 minutes and was jointly produced by iQIYI and DaMeng Innovation. The production used […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese streaming platform iQIYI has launched Qitan: Paper Blade Across the Wasteland (《奇谭：纸刃渡荒墟》), which it describes as the first AIGC online story film in China to receive a Network Drama/Film Distribution License and go online. The film runs for more than 60 minutes and was jointly produced by iQIYI and DaMeng Innovation.

The production used AI across the creative and production process, including concept development, prompt workflows, visual asset generation, AI-assisted image transfer, 4K output, and postproduction. A team of more than 20 people created over 200 characters and other visual assets for the film.

The film is the first installment of iQIYI’s planned Qitan trilogy, which uses Chinese supernatural folklore as its creative foundation. The project gives iQIYI a long-form test of whether AIGC can support production workflows beyond short videos and promotional content. [IT Home, in Chinese]

item.title: Ford and Geely reportedly reach deal to produce EVs at Spanish plant
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Ford Motor and China’s Geely have reportedly reached an agreement for Geely to produce electric vehicles at Ford’s Almussafes plant near Valencia, Spain, Reuters reported, citing Spanish newspaper ABC. The deal is expected to be announced during a July 23 visit to the factory by Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Ford Europe President Jim […]
item.content:encoded:
Ford Motor and China’s Geely have reportedly reached an agreement for Geely to produce electric vehicles at Ford’s Almussafes plant near Valencia, Spain, Reuters reported, citing Spanish newspaper ABC. The deal is expected to be announced during a July 23 visit to the factory by Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Ford Europe President Jim Baumbick.

ABC reported that Geely could produce its EX2 electric vehicle at the plant. The arrangement would give Geely a manufacturing base inside the European Union, helping it gain direct access to the European market and potentially avoid tariffs on EVs imported from China.

For Ford, shared use of the factory could reduce fixed costs and help preserve jobs and production at Almussafes, whose future has been affected by the phaseout of several models and its reliance on the Kuga. [Reuters]

item.title: DeepSeek puts AGI research ahead of products and commercial growth
item.dc:creator: Penghui LI
item.description: According to a report by IT Home, DeepSeek, the Chinese AI developer behind the open-source R1 reasoning model, is prioritizing artificial general intelligence (AGI) research over building a consumer platform or maximizing near-term revenue. The report is based on a circulated transcript of a four-hour investor meeting involving founder Liang Wenfeng. Why it matters: The […]
item.content:encoded:
According to a report by IT Home, DeepSeek, the Chinese AI developer behind the open-source R1 reasoning model, is prioritizing artificial general intelligence (AGI) research over building a consumer platform or maximizing near-term revenue. The report is based on a circulated transcript of a four-hour investor meeting involving founder Liang Wenfeng.

Why it matters: The reported strategy presents DeepSeek as a research-led AI developer whose products and commercial services are secondary outcomes of its AGI work.

  • DeepSeek’s open-source reasoning models have drawn global attention by combining strong performance with relatively low-cost development.
  • Liang reportedly said DeepSeek is not focused on competing for consumer traffic, enterprise revenue, or becoming the next ByteDance or Tencent.
  • The approach helps explain the company’s emphasis on open-source models, lower API prices, and improvements in training and inference efficiency.
  • It also distinguishes DeepSeek from AI companies that are building standalone products and enterprise businesses around their models.

Details: Liang reportedly sees continual learning as the next major technical challenge after AI agents.

  • He described a development path from chain-of-thought reasoning to AI agents, continual learning, and eventually embodied intelligence.
  • Current AI systems generally need users to provide the relevant context again for each task. Liang reportedly believes future models should be able to learn continuously from experience.
  • Coding agents are a priority because they could improve both DeepSeek’s products and its own research process.
  • DeepSeek is not prioritizing 3D generation, video generation, or world models, which Liang reportedly views as useful applications but not central to the development of intelligence.
  • Multimodal capabilities will still be developed for consumer and enterprise products.
  • Liang reportedly said DeepSeek’s API pricing is designed to recover hardware costs in around 10 months, rather than maximize profit.
  • DeepSeek also reportedly plans to keep its strongest models open source and use the same models internally and externally, rather than release weaker versions to the public.

Context: DeepSeek’s public profile has been shaped by its open-source and low-cost approach, but the reported comments suggest that the company still sees itself primarily as an AI research organization.

  • Consumer products, enterprise services, and API revenue are reportedly intended to support the company’s longer-term technical goals.
  • The comments offer a view into how DeepSeek may be positioning itself as the AI industry shifts from model releases toward agents and systems that can assist with AI research.
